description: Clamp a number between a minimum and maximum value
prompt: Write a JavaScript ES module that exports a function `clamp(value, min, max)` which restricts `value` to be within the range `[min, max]`. If `value` is less than `min`, return `min`. If `value` is greater than `max`, return `max`. Otherwise return `value`. Export as named export.

tests.name: handles value equal to bounds
tests.code: assert(mod.clamp(0, 0, 10) === 0, 'Expected 0 at lower bound'); assert(mod.clamp(10, 0, 10) === 10, 'Expected 10 at upper bound');

tests.name: handles negative ranges
tests.code: assert(mod.clamp(-3, -10, -1) === -3, 'Expected -3 in negative range'); assert(mod.clamp(0, -10, -1) === -1, 'Expected -1 for value above negative max');
